title: NBA Daily Fast Break
subtitle: Your comprehensive NBA news digest for March 13, 2026

items.headline: SGA Shatters Wilt's Scoring Record!
items.summary: OKC Thunder star Shai Gilgeous-Alexander made history last night, extending his incredible streak to 127 consecutive games with at least 20 points, breaking Wilt Chamberlain's long-standing NBA record. He delivered 35 points in OKC's thrilling 104-102 victory over the Boston Celtics.

items.headline: Doncic Explodes for 51 as LeBron Returns for Lakers!
items.summary: Luka Doncic erupted for a monstrous 51 points, 10 rebounds, and 9 assists, fueling the Los Angeles Lakers to a 142-130 win over the Chicago Bulls. The stellar performance coincided with LeBron James' return to action, who added 18 points after missing three games due to injuries.

items.headline: Wemby Sidelined with Ankle Soreness, Spurs Fall to Nuggets
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs phenom Victor Wembanyama missed Thursday night's game against the Denver Nuggets due to right ankle soreness. The Spurs, without their star who was recently named Western Conference Player of the Week (ending March 8), fell 136-131. Coach Mitch Johnson stated the injury is not a long-term concern.

items.headline: Magic Extend Win Streak Over Cavs
items.summary: Desmond Bane led the Magic with 35 points, pushing Orlando's winning streak to five games. James Harden had 30 for the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Pelicans Top Raptors in High-Scoring Affair
items.summary: Trey Murphy III scored 28 points for the Pelicans, who secured their second consecutive win. Immanuel Quickley paced the Raptors with 25 points.

items.headline: Knicks Overcome Jazz Behind Brunson's 28
items.summary: Jalen Brunson's 28 points spearheaded the Knicks' comeback victory over the Jazz. Brice Sensabaugh put up 29 points for Utah.

items.headline: Nuggets Dominate Rockets; Jokic Triple-Double
items.summary: Jamal Murray had 30 points and Nikola Jokic recorded a triple-double (16 points, 12 rebounds, 13 assists) as the Nuggets routed the Rockets.

items.headline: Hornets Sting Kings with Ball's 30 Points
items.summary: LaMelo Ball dropped 30 points to lead the Hornets past DeMar DeRozan's 39-point effort for the Kings.

items.headline: Kawhi's 45 Powers Clippers Over Timberwolves
items.summary: Kawhi Leonard exploded for 45 points, guiding the Clippers to a dominant win. Anthony Edwards led the Timberwolves with 36 points.

items.headline: Thunder Edge Celtics in Record-Breaking Night
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander broke Wilt Chamberlain's 20-point streak record with 35 points, as Chet Holmgren's late free throws sealed a close victory over an undermanned Celtics squad.

items.headline: Hawks' Win Streak Hits Eight Against Nets
items.summary: Jalen Johnson nearly notched a triple-double (21 points, 9 rebounds, 9 assists) as the Hawks extended the league's longest current winning streak to eight games. Josh Minott had a career-high 24 for Brooklyn.

items.headline: Heat Continue Hot Streak, Beat Bucks
items.summary: Pelle Larsson scored 28 points and Bam Adebayo added 21 as the Heat won their seventh consecutive game.

items.headline: Pistons vs. 76ers
items.summary: The Detroit Pistons host the Philadelphia 76ers this morning.

items.headline: Pacers vs. Suns
items.summary: The Indiana Pacers take on the Phoenix Suns.

items.headline: Magic vs. Wizards
items.summary: The Orlando Magic welcome the Washington Wizards.

items.headline: Hawks vs. Nets
items.summary: Fresh off an eight-game winning streak, the Atlanta Hawks face the Brooklyn Nets again.

items.headline: Heat vs. Bucks
items.summary: The Miami Heat look to extend their seven-game winning streak against the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: Grizzlies vs. Mavericks
items.summary: The Memphis Grizzlies battle the Dallas Mavericks.

items.headline: Trail Blazers vs. Jazz
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers, featuring Deni Avdija, will square off against the Utah Jazz tonight.

items.headline: Luka Doncic (LAL)
items.statContext: Dominated the Bulls in a massive offensive display.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard (LAC)
items.statContext: Led the Clippers' scoring barrage against the Timberwolves.

items.headline: DeMar DeRozan (SAC)
items.statContext: Impressive scoring effort for the Kings in their loss to the Hornets.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (OKC)
items.statContext: Broke Wilt Chamberlain's record for consecutive 20-point games.

items.headline: Anthony Edwards (MIN)
items.statContext: Paced the Timberwolves in their game against the Clippers.

items.headline: LaMelo Ball (CHA)
items.statContext: Fueled the Hornets to a victory over the Kings.

items.headline: Jamal Murray (DEN)
items.statContext: Led the Nuggets in scoring during their dominant win.

items.headline: Austin Reaves (LAL)
items.statContext: Chipped in a significant scoring output for the Lakers.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic (DEN)
items.statContext: Another classic all-around performance from the reigning MVP candidate.

items.headline: Deni Avdija (POR)
items.statContext: The Israeli forward is having a career year and will be a player to watch in Portland's upcoming game against Utah.

items.headline: Torrey Craig Eyeing NBA Return Post-NBL Season
items.summary: Sydney Kings star Torrey Craig is reportedly in discussions with NBA teams for a potential return to the league after his NBL season concludes. Craig, a veteran of over 500 NBA games, is focused on winning a championship with the Kings first.

items.headline: Key Injury Updates Across the League
items.summary: Victor Wembanyama (SAS) missed Thursday's game with right ankle soreness but is not expected to be out long-term. LeBron James (LAL) successfully returned from a hip contusion and foot arthritis. Other notable absences for upcoming games include Tyrese Haliburton (IND), Jarrett Allen (CLE), and Ausar Thompson (DET).

items.headline: Playoff Picture Heating Up: Standings & Streaks
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder (52-15) lead the Western Conference, closely followed by the San Antonio Spurs (48-17). In the East, the Detroit Pistons (46-18) hold the top spot. Several teams are on hot streaks: the Hawks (8 wins), Heat (7 wins), Thunder (7 wins), Magic (6 wins), and Lakers (4 wins). The Sacramento Kings and Indiana Pacers have been officially eliminated from playoff contention.
